input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Report a verbalized confidence and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Per-region surrogate competence not characterized: a known high-variance-flow region is not flagged as locally out-of-competence even when the input is globally in-envelope. The package establishes global applicability but not local applicability for the region the COU depends on.
fired_pattern.standard_anchor: ASME V&V 40-2018 applicability; credibility bounded to the validated domain

case_context.cou: Surrogate informs a drag-sensitivity decision; flow in the wheel-housing region contributes to the quantity of interest
case_context.decision_consequence: design change carried forward on surrogate result

answer_key.gold_mechanism: Global in-envelope status does not establish local competence in a known high-variance region; per-region characterization is absent for a region the COU depends on.
forbidden_claims: asserts the surrogate is inaccurate or wrong in the wheel-housing (a correctness verdict the flag does not support)
treats the 41st-percentile global pass as competence evidence for the region
recommends accepting on the basis of the global band

label_provenance.external_grounding: CarBench (arXiv:2512.07847) characterizes wheel-housing as a high-variance regime where surrogates struggle locally; DrivAerML solver-truth eval shows elevated regional error in the wheel-housing despite global in-envelope status.
